narrative_progress.label: build story command merge
narrative_progress.chunk: Merged the standalone phase surface into the plan and complete workflows so phase decomposition now follows the same optional flag pattern as the research flow. The shipped change added with-phases handling, wired a phase_decomposition block into workflow assets, removed obsolete command files, and updated the primary command docs. This paragraph is about what changed in the system, not rollout sequencing.

narrative_progress.label: new generator capability
narrative_progress.chunk: Packet-local changelog generation now lives beside the implementation-summary workflow. The generator resolves packet roots versus child phases, reads packet documents such as spec.md, tasks.md, and checklist.md, and derives final changelog structure from those sources instead of asking operators to hand-assemble markdown. The point of this chunk is the new capability and its moving parts.

narrative_progress.label: runtime fix concrete delta
narrative_progress.chunk: The search pipeline now keeps folder discovery as a scoring boost instead of promoting the discovered folder into an exact-match filter, and stage one no longer treats sessionId as a governance scope boundary. Together those changes unblock focused-mode searches that were returning zero candidates. The chunk is about the substantive runtime correction and the precise behavior that changed.

narrative_progress.label: foundation refactor contract surface
narrative_progress.chunk: Phase one established a canonical review manifest, normalized severity handling, and split shared review doctrine from single-pass UX rules. The resulting package now has one source of truth for the review-mode contract and a narrower shared core that downstream review agents can reuse. This explains structural work that now exists in the repository.

narrative_progress.label: packet capability expansion
narrative_progress.chunk: The canonical generator now exposes nested changelog output for packet roots and child phases from one shared runtime contract instead of scattering changelog logic across packet folders. It resolves the right output surface for both packet roots and child phases, keeps filename derivation deterministic, and centralizes the output structure in one capability. The emphasis is on the new behavior that now exists in the system.

narrative_delivery.label: sequencing paragraph
narrative_delivery.chunk: The work was delivered in three passes: first analyze existing packet examples, then add the generator and templates, then align command, skill, and reference surfaces in the same packet. Closure only happened after the first Vitest failure exposed template-path resolution drift and the generator was patched. This is about delivery sequence and verification gating, not feature substance.

narrative_delivery.label: verification first rollout
narrative_delivery.chunk: Verification stayed intentionally narrow and honest. The scripts workspace build was rerun after the generator fix, a focused Vitest suite covered both packet-root and phase-child output, and only then were the packet docs synchronized so the workflow change was auditable. The distinctive cue is delivery mechanics and evidence path.

narrative_delivery.label: staged runtime remediation
narrative_delivery.chunk: Attempt one confirmed that semantic search returned results again, then attempt two kept the work pending until the MCP server restart could happen and the focused-mode fix could be rechecked in order. The implementation compiled before handover, but release was deliberately blocked by the verification gate and only then could the delivery story close. The emphasis is on sequencing, gating, and verification order.

narrative_delivery.label: same pass alignment
narrative_delivery.chunk: Commands, templates, validators, and reference docs were updated together in the same pass because the packet could only ship after every surface pointed at the same runtime truth. Documentation was not released before runtime parity, and verification checks happened before the final delivery step. That same-pass, verify-before-release language is a delivery pattern.

narrative_delivery.label: feature flag rollout shape
narrative_delivery.chunk: The recommended implementation path keeps the new writer behind a dual-write feature flag, runs shadow comparison before any default flip, and treats schema migration plus archive weighting as Gate B rather than post-merge cleanup. This chunk is about rollout discipline, gates, and deployment sequencing.

decision.label: playbook first adr
decision.chunk: We chose to build the manual testing playbook first and state the missing feature catalog explicitly rather than invent a companion package. The future playbook can ship truthfully today, while any catalog linkage is deferred to a later decision. Alternatives considered were catalog-first and dual-package release, both rejected because they expand scope and delay the operator-facing validation surface.

decision.label: integrated package shape
decision.chunk: We chose the integrated root-guidance package shape and mapped the approved scenario inventory into six named category folders. The reason is structural: it is the only option that preserves the shipped contract and the approved scenario order at the same time. The chunk includes alternatives, rationale, and rollback language.

decision.label: orchestrator owns side effects
decision.chunk: We chose to keep all journal write operations in the orchestrator, never in the proposal agent. If the agent can emit journal entries directly, it is no longer honestly proposal-only, which breaks the safety guarantee of bounded improvement loops. The consequence is that the orchestrator must call emit hooks at every lifecycle boundary.

decision.label: namespace reuse adr
decision.chunk: We chose to reuse the existing coverage-graph infrastructure by namespacing all writes with loop_type improvement instead of building a second graph system. Separate infrastructure would isolate data but duplicate persistence, query, and maintenance burden. Namespace reuse keeps the API surface stable while preserving data isolation.

decision.label: backward compatible defaults
decision.chunk: We chose to make all new configuration fields optional with documented defaults so the skill behaves exactly like its pre-phase version when fields are absent. The explicit goal is backward compatibility during rollout. The alternative would have been a mandatory config migration, but that would turn a bounded improvement packet into a breaking-change packet.

handover_state.label: continuation header progress
handover_state.chunk: Current state for the continuation: research is complete, the foundation phase is complete, and later phases were dispatched as parallel agents. The blocker is verification of those agent outputs before closure. Next session work is to inspect git diff, review the generated changes, and then finish the remaining template update. The emphasis is the stop-state and resume path, not the command itself.

handover_state.label: current state blocker
handover_state.chunk: Current state: implementation fixes are compiled, the MCP server was killed to force a clean restart, and runtime verification must happen in a fresh session before the packet can be closed. Active files are the focused handler fix and the stage one and stage two search pipeline modules. The handoff is about what is live right now and what blocks closure.

handover_state.label: pending work checklist
handover_state.chunk: Immediate next session work: verify the Phase two and three agent output, verify the Phase four and five agent output, update the deep-review strategy template, run git diff, and then commit. Deferred work remains explicitly separated from immediate work. The phrasing is operational and time-bound.

handover_state.label: quick start resume instructions
handover_state.chunk: To resume in the next session, start from the current state recorded here and review the handover's listed files in order: the focused handler fix, the stage one scope fix, the stage two graph diagnostic warning, and the packet docs that still need verification evidence. The resume command is part of the restart recipe, but the durable content is the stop-state and file order for picking up where work paused.

handover_state.label: risks and effort estimate
handover_state.chunk: Current blockers are limited to runtime restart verification, but there is still risk that removing sessionId from scope enforcement has an unintended side effect in consumers that expected session-scoped filtering. Estimated remaining effort is about thirty minutes for verification, documentation closure, memory save, and commit.

research_finding.label: smallest correct recommendation
research_finding.chunk: The repo already has the needed command docs; the actual discoverability problem is that the quick reference spends its prime space on documentation mechanics instead of a first-touch command shortlist. The smallest correct recommendation is a compact start-here command block, not a new generated registry.

research_finding.label: architecture conclusion
research_finding.chunk: The compact code graph work should use a clean-room tree-sitter plus SQLite approach rather than a full graph database. The research across tooling and academic sources points to a hybrid retrieval system: embeddings for recall, structural graph signals for precision, and a compact projection layer for model-facing context. This is a research synthesis chunk.

research_finding.label: overlap matrix insight
research_finding.chunk: Across the sampled memory corpus, the majority of bytes are high-overlap narrative that maps cleanly to implementation-summary, decision-record, handover, and tasks. The unique long-term value is concentrated in metadata, preflight baselines, and postflight learning deltas. That finding narrows the thin continuity layer to a small machine-owned surface.

research_finding.label: archive viability finding
research_finding.chunk: The bounded-archive strategy is obsolete after the canonical continuity cleanup. Search treats every retained row as equally fresh, and archived-hit telemetry no longer exists because the archived tier is no longer load-bearing.

research_finding.label: prerequisite blocker finding
research_finding.chunk: The biggest migration blocker is not schema complexity; it is the small set of root packets whose memory files are still the only narrative artifact. If those packets are archived before root-level canonical docs are backfilled, the implementation story is lost. This identifies a hidden dependency and reframes the order of operations.

task_update.label: completed implementation slice
task_update.chunk: [x] T004 Add with-phases flag to the plan command. [x] T005 Add the phase decomposition section to the plan command. [x] T008 Wire phase_decomposition into the auto plan workflow asset. These lines are direct status mutations against scoped task IDs.

task_update.label: verification still open
task_update.chunk: [x] T015 Update README text to remove the phase command row. [x] T016 Update quick reference wording. [ ] T017 Verify the plan command without with-phases works unchanged. Completion criteria remain open until manual verification passes.

task_update.label: packet wide completion
task_update.chunk: [x] T012 Fix template path resolution in the generator after the first test failure. [x] T013 Run scripts build verification. [x] T014 Run focused nested changelog Vitest verification. [x] T015 Update packet documentation to match the shipped workflow.

task_update.label: structural parent packet work
task_update.chunk: [ ] T004 Add the minimal summary of the child phase to the root packet. [ ] T005 Ensure the parent-child relationship resolves through the child spec. [ ] T006 Keep parent scope structural only. The wording is sparse on purpose because task updates often name exact to-dos without rationale paragraphs.

task_update.label: completion criteria block
task_update.chunk: [x] All implementation and workflow-alignment tasks marked complete. [x] No blocked tasks remain in this packet. [x] Verification captured in implementation-summary and checklist. This mutates packet status directly even though it looks summary-like.

metadata_only.label: frontmatter-only classification
metadata_only.chunk: title: Implementation Summary Nested Changelog Per Spec; trigger_phrases: implementation summary, nested changelog, phase changelog, 025; importance_tier: important; contextType: implementation. There is no session narrative here, only retrieval metadata that improves indexing and ranking.

metadata_only.label: continuity block
metadata_only.chunk: _memory.continuity recent_action phase two fixes compiled; next_safe_action restart MCP server and rerun focused-mode query; blockers server disconnected after pkill; completion_pct 95; fingerprint sha256 value. This is thin continuity state: machine-owned, highly structured, and intentionally compact.

metadata_only.label: causal and decay payload
metadata_only.chunk: memory_classification memory_type semantic; half_life_days 365; decay_factors base_decay_rate 0.9981; access_boost_factor 0.1; causal_links supersedes F-017-004; provenance session_id 018-impl-design. The chunk is valuable to retrieval, but it is not readable narrative.

metadata_only.label: preflight baseline
metadata_only.chunk: preflight knowledgeScore 42; uncertaintyScore 71; contextScore 38; knowledgeGaps root packet backfill count, dedup key shape, resume fallback latency; taskId implementation. This belongs in continuity metadata even though it captures session state.

metadata_only.label: postflight learning delta
metadata_only.chunk: postflight knowledgeScore 88; uncertaintyScore 24; contextScore 84; gapsClosed routing overlap confirmed, schema blocker isolated; newGapsDiscovered prototype confusion tests still needed; learningIndex 0.74. The structure is score-heavy and field-heavy.

drop.label: conversation transcript
drop.chunk: 2026-04-11 user continue anyway and keep scope on packet 026. 2026-04-11 assistant found two more findings. 2026-04-11 tool git diff name only. 2026-04-11 assistant applying targeted fix. This is timestamped dialogue and tool telemetry.

drop.label: recovery hints boilerplate
drop.chunk: Recovery scenarios context loss run resume, state mismatch check git status, memory not found call memory_search, stale context compare timestamps, diagnostic commands list memories, verify file integrity, force re-index. This is generic operator boilerplate repeated across many memory files.

drop.label: table of contents wrapper
drop.chunk: Table of contents continue session, project state snapshot, overview, detailed changes, decisions, conversation, recovery hints, memory metadata. The chunk exists only to help a human scan a long legacy memory file and carries almost no semantic retrieval value by itself.

drop.label: auto truncated summary
drop.chunk: Retroactive body contains auto-truncated summary text from the memory generator. Ellipsis markers are known truncation points, not typos. Recent deep research improving review logic in sk-deep-research auto-truncated. This looks summary-like but the truncation marker and repair-note framing make it poor canonical content.

drop.label: raw tool telemetry
drop.chunk: Captured file count 8. Files modified memory-context.ts, stage1-candidate-gen.ts, stage2-fusion.ts, types.ts. Tool executions zero. Repository state clean. Channel system-speckit 026-graph-and-context-optimization. On its own this is operational telemetry rather than canonical content.
